clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL) and creatinine elevation meets Sepsis-3 criteria. Thrombocytopenia (<100 K/uL) persists across three measurements with SOFA coagulation scoring threshold crossed. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) and hemoglobin reduction (9.8 g/dL) suggest hepatic and hematologic involvement. AKI Stage 1 is present with subsequent excluded Stage 3 creatinine spike (8.9 mg/dL) likely representing measurement artifact.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus SOFA increase ≥2
Creatinine elevation meets KDIGO Stage 1 criteria (≥0.3 mg/dL increase in 48h)
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or urine output <0.5 mL/kg/hr ≥6h

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL crosses SOFA coagulation scoring threshold
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but no documentation of vasopressor-dependent hypotension or fluid resuscitation status
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent MAP ≥65 mmHg + lactate >2 mmol/L post-fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate sepsis bundle: broad-spectrum antibiotics, source control investigation, and hemodynamic monitoring
Order blood cultures and infectious workup for suspected sepsis source
Monitor AKI progression with hourly urine output and serial creatinine
Consider platelet transfusion if thrombocytopenia <50 K/uL with bleeding risk
Reassess fluid status given elevated lactate and WBC
doctor_handoff: Review cultures/imaging for sepsis source, confirm vasopressor requirements for septic shock exclusion, and evaluate for AKI reversal strategies

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL
AKI Stage 1 (Cr 2.1 mg/dL) and platelets 95 K/uL
Lactate peaks at 4.2 mmol/L with platelets 88 K/uL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
